content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
(916) 322~2 
Michael Clasen 
Assistant City Attorney 
City of Hawthorne 
4455 W. 126th Street 
Hawthorne, CA 
90250 
Dear MSe Clasen: 
Administration 
•• 
executive/Legal 
•• 
Enforcement 
•• Statements of Economic Interesf 
322-.5660 
322·5901 
322-6441 
322-6444 
February 5, 1985 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-85~022 
This letter is sent to confirm our telephone conversation 
concerning Government Code Section 91013(c). 
This section 
generally provides for the assessment of late filing penalties 
by filing officers. 
Subsection (c) states in part: 
(c) • • • No liability under this section shall 
exceed the cumulative amount stated in the late 
statement or report, or one hundred dollars ($100), 
whichever is greater. 
You asked, with respect to campaign statements filed 
pursuant to Chapter 4 of the Political Reform Act, how we 
interpret the term "cumulative amount." 
This term refers to the 
cumulative amount of the contributions for the period covered by 
the statement or the cumulative amount of the expenditures for 
the period covered by the statement, whichever is greater. 
If I can be of further assistance, please feel free to 
contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
DMF:plh 
Sincerely, 
c4iL~,-~:CL ;:u~ 
Diane Maura Fishburn 
Staff Counsel 
Legal Division

sections.question: With respect to campaign statements filed pursuant to Chapter 4 of the Political Reform Act, how should the term 'cumulative amount' in Government Code Section 91013(c) be interpreted?
sections.conclusion: This term refers to the cumulative amount of the contributions for the period covered by the statement or the cumulative amount of the expenditures for the period covered by the statement, whichever is greater.
sections.question_synthetic: How should the FPPC interpret the term 'cumulative amount' under Government Code Section 91013(c) regarding late filing penalties for campaign statements?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The 'cumulative amount' referenced in Government Code Section 91013(c) means the greater of either the total contributions or total expenditures for the period covered by the late campaign statement.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Telephone conversation confirmed via letter; clear Q&C structure present. Document is dated February 5, 1985 with File No. A-85-022.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: With respect to campaign statements filed pursuant to Chapter 4 of the Political Reform Act, how should the term 'cumulative amount' in Government Code Section 91013(c) be interpreted?

CONCLUSION: This term refers to the cumulative amount of the contributions for the period covered by the statement or the cumulative amount of the expenditures for the period covered by the statement, whichever is greater.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to the City of Hawthorne's Assistant City Attorney regarding the interpretation of 'cumulative amount' under Government Code Section 91013(c) for assessing late filing penalties on campaign statements. The FPPC advises that this term refers to whichever is greater: cumulative contributions or cumulative expenditures for the statement period.
